references.notes: This verse contains the pre-Islamic oath formula "لعمرك" (by your life), which is a common form of swearing.
The verse explicitly refers to "قداح بأيدي ياسر" (arrows in the hands of a lot-caster), indicating the pre-Islamic practice of divination by arrows (azlām).
The term "نوح" (lamentation) refers to a pre-Islamic mourning rite for the deceased.
The phrase "ليلة نحس" (an unlucky night) reflects a pre-Islamic belief in inauspicious times or omens.
This verse explicitly mentions "جن" (jinn), which are non-divine supernatural entities in pre-Islamic Arabian belief.
The mention of "الشعرى" (Sirius) as influencing a "day" indicates a pre-Islamic astral belief about the influence of stars on earthly events or weather.
